title: Contact
text:
The best way to reach me is through the channels below.
For questions or issues with a specific tool, please open a GitHub Issue in the relevant repository — that keeps the conversation visible and searchable for other users.


Email: lifestyle_Zion@protonmail.com
GitHub: github.com/rinecology
LinkedIn: linkedin.com/in/zhangyuplantecologist

title: About
text:
I am a Registered Professional Forester (RPF) and forest analyst with over 15 years of combined experience in academic ecological research and applied forest management in Ontario. My career spans from peer-reviewed global-scale ecological research to frontline forest management planning across three Ontario MNR districts.
Since joining the Ontario public service in 2017, I have been a member of FMP planning teams for seven Forest Management Units across Northwest, Northeast, and Southern Ontario — covering base model setup, yield curve fitting, long-term management direction, and stakeholder consultation including Indigenous community engagement. My current focus is on wood supply modeling, spatial data tool development, and analytical automation to support upcoming FMP planning cycles in the northeast GLSL forest region.
The tools on this site were built out of practical necessity — to fill gaps in existing workflows for data validation, yield curve modeling, LiDAR analysis, and forest unit classification.

title: About
text:
Research Background (2008–2017)
Prior to joining the Ontario public service, I was an active researcher in boreal forest ecosystem dynamics, with a focus on biodiversity–productivity relationships across global forest biomes. My postdoctoral work was conducted at Lakehead University and the South China Botanical Garden, Chinese Academy of Sciences.
My 5 first-author papers have accumulated 1,259 citations (as of April 2026, per Semantic Scholar), averaging ~252 citations per paper. The 2012 Journal of Ecology meta-analysis alone has been cited by primary research papers published in Science (2016) and Nature (2017).

text:

Forest inventory analysis (FRI/PCI/BMI/OPI — Ontario MNRF standards)
Explicit spatial forest estate modeling (e.g., Woodstock and FSOS)
LiDAR data processing and 3D visualization
Yield curve modeling (Advanced user of SLYM & BMI-based fitting)
Interactive web application development (R/Shiny, JavaScript)
Spatial data workflows and GIS automation (ArcGIS Pro, Python, R)
Data visualization and reporting (Power BI, Quarto, Leaflet)

title: Tools & Applications
text: A collection of open-source tools developed for forest inventory analysis, data quality validation, and spatial decision-support. Each tool is available as a distribution package — see individual repository links below.

title: Tools & Applications
text:
JavaScript Applications

ForestUnit JS — Ontario Forest Unit Analysis Tool

Description:
SQL-style forest unit assignment running 100% in the browser — no server, no installation. Ships with 7 bundled YAML rule sets (122+ rules): NER Boreal (TN021 2006, 2017–2019, ROD2023, SRNV2023, SRNV SPCOMP ONLY, SRNV2024) and GLSL SFU v1.03.01.23. Parses raw SPCOMP strings, assigns SFUs, and produces CSV + HTML reports. Key feature: cross-rule-set comparison mode with a diverging bar chart showing area delta vs a chosen baseline. ~330KB bundle, works offline.
Tech: JavaScript (ES2020+), Vite, Chart.js
Live Demo: rinecology.github.io/forest-unit-js/
Source: Hosted in this portfolio repo under /forest-unit-js/

title: Tools & Applications
text:
R / Shiny Applications

Forest Classification Analyzer

Description:
R/Shiny companion to ForestUnit JS, built for batch and multi-scenario work. Killer feature: simultaneously compare 2–5 classification scenarios side-by-side with confusion matrices, agreement analysis, and area impact assessment — immediately surface which records disagree and why. Supports NER, NWR, and GLSL regions; SFU, FMG, and PLANFU output types; First/Best/All Match modes. Accepts CSV, DBF, and Shapefile uploads up to 500 MB. Custom YAML upload enables testing experimental rule sets before committing them.
Tech: R, Shiny
Version: 1.0.0
Source: github.com/rinecology/forest-unit-shiny-dist
Download: Available as a zip via GitHub Releases in the above repo



LOGIC_CHECKER — Forest Inventory Data Quality Reporting System

Description:
Comprehensive validation and reporting system for Ontario forest inventory data (FRI/PCI/BMI/OPI). Implements 156 production-ready validation rules with spatial mapping integration. Supports CSV and Shapefile ZIP upload, severity-colored centroid mapping, and both an interactive Shiny app and a PowerShell batch mode.
Tech: R, Shiny, Leaflet, PowerShell
Version: 6.2 (March 2026)
Source: github.com/rinecology/logic-checker-dist
Download: Available as a zip via GitHub Releases in the above repo



FRI LiDAR Toolkit

Description:
R-based toolkit for processing and visualizing LiDAR-derived datasets from Ontario’s FRI Leaf-ON LiDAR program. Features batch CHM tile download from Ontario GeoHub, virtual mosaic clipping (no ArcGIS), side-by-side FRI vs LiDAR comparison, crown delineation, and 3D point cloud visualization via WebGL.
Tech: R, Shiny, terra, lidR, WebGL
Source: github.com/rinecology/fri-lidar-dist
Download: Available as a zip via GitHub Releases in the above repo

title: Tools & Applications
text:
R Pipeline Frameworks

SLYM Companion — Stand Level Yield Curve Modeling Suite

Description:
An integrated suite of four specialized tools for yield curve modeling and result processing: SLYM Processor, SLYM Runner, SLYM Auto Runner, and SLYM Cutter. Covers model parameter setup, batch execution, and output visualization. Managed template preparation (FGS/PRSNT subsetting, SI imputation, BA enrichment) is handled upstream by FRI DataKit.
Tech: R, Shiny, PowerShell
Source: github.com/rinecology/slym-companion-dist
Download: Available as a zip via GitHub Releases in the above repo



FRI DataKit — Forest Inventory Data Preparation Suite

Description:
A multi-function Shiny app for forest inventory data wrangling and pre-modeling preparation. Started as an NER managed yield curve template builder and grew into a comprehensive toolkit: silviculture-based subsetting (FGS/PRSNT/PRSNT_NAT), area-weighted attribute analysis, SI imputation, STKG and BA enrichment, leading species / AU_YC parsing with hierarchical fallback logic, and multi-region forest unit classification (NER/NWR/GLSL for SFU/FMG/PLANFU). Outputs feed directly into SLYM managed template generation.
Tech: R, Shiny, YAML-driven rules
Version: 0.2.0 (February 2026)
Source: github.com/rinecology/fri-datakit-dist
Download: Available as a zip via GitHub Releases in the above repo
